summary: McKenzie reports that his landing page redesign dramatically improved AdWords conversion rates from ~21-22% to 28%. The redesign focused on a clear 'One True Goal' button that captured over 180 clicks per 1,000 visitors. This improvement effectively reduces his customer acquisition cost by about 25%. He anticipates Google's Conversion Optimizer will respond by increasing his CPC bids, which should drive even more impressions and conversions.
key_points: A focused landing page with a single clear call-to-action ('One True Goal' button) boosted conversion rates from ~22% to 28%
A 28% conversion rate means customer acquisition costs dropped by roughly 25%, significant since AdWords was his largest expense
Google's Conversion Optimizer creates a positive feedback loop: better conversion rates lead to higher bids, more impressions, and more conversions
Heatmap tools like CrazyEgg provided the visual evidence to measure where users actually clicked
money_quotes: I am almost embarassed how well this is working.
For the last 1,000 visitors, the One True Goal button got over 180 clicks.
The conversion rate is about 28%. Yowza!
The cost of customer acquisition for me just declined by about 25%, which is good because AdWords is my largest expense by far.
This means that the same number of impressions results in a third more trial downloads for me, and hopefully thus a third more sales.
key_insight: A focused landing page redesign with a single clear call-to-action can dramatically improve conversion rates, creating a compounding effect through lower acquisition costs and algorithmic bid optimization.
